title: grove doctor - core::doctor report + CLI verb (checks, --json, exit code)

plan.objective: Implement grove doctor with a new core::harness module (sentinel constants + mode→content tables), core::doctor (pure diagnose with universal + explore checks), and Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
key_changes: New core/src/harness.rs: GROVE_START, GROVE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY, expected_mcp_args(Mode), expected_claude_marker(Mode), agents_md_expected(Mode) — resolves constant-ownership blocker.
cli/src/init.rs refactored: remove local CLAUDE_START/CLAUDE_END/MCP_SERVER_KEY consts; import from grove_core::harness — sentinel strings live in exactly one place.
New core/src/doctor.rs: Status{Ok,Warn,Fail,Info}, Check{group,name,status,detail,hint}, Report{mode,checks}, Report::ok(), diagnose(root,force)->Report — pure read-only.
core/src/lib.rs: add pub mod harness and pub mod doctor.
Universal checks: grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, four harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.
Harness sub-checks use harness::expected_mcp_args/expected_claude_marker/agents_md_expected; validate on-disk files vs declared mode; Fail on drift.
Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only): explore_config_valid, provider_reachable, model_served, allowed_tools_known, tap_config.
cli/src/main.rs: Cmd::Doctor{path,explore,standard}; dispatch arm; grouped ✓/⚠/✗ human output or --json; exit code from Report::ok().
Unit tests in core/src/doctor.rs build all fixtures by hand (tempdir + std::fs::write) — resolves test-strategy blocker (no cross-crate private helpers).
Harness drift matrix: per-mode clean fixture → all harness checks Ok; selected drift scenarios → correct Fail/Warn.
warn_only_report_exits_zero: Report::ok() true when only Warn/Info present — AC 9 warning boundary (per reviewer advisory).
Integration tests: doctor --help, clean project exits 0, --json parses, induced drift exits 1 under both output modes.

review_plan.objective: Re-reviewed the revised grove doctor plan (core::harness + core::doctor + Cmd::Doctor) to confirm the two prior blockers and one advisory were resolved and all reused seams exist.
key_changes: Blocker 1 (constant ownership) resolved: new core/src/harness.rs is single source of truth; init.rs migrates to import it — no core->cli cycle, no duplication.
Verified on-disk init.rs owns CLAUDE_START/END (l.20-21) and MCP_SERVER_KEY (l.79); plan's per-mode markers match real steering blocks (outline/skill/explore).
Blocker 2 (cross-crate private test helpers) resolved: unit tests build tempdir fixtures via std::fs::write, no reconcile_harness/seed_lock imports.
Advisory (warn-only exits zero) incorporated as warn_only_report_exits_zero; Report::ok() correctly keys only off Status::Fail.
Seam active_mode(root, ModeChoice)->Mode confirmed (config.rs:291); ModeChoice None/ForceExplore/ForceStandard matches --explore/--standard precedence.
verify_lock (registry.rs:465), health_probe + HealthError{Unreachable,ModelMissing} (client.rs), list_models, ExploreConfig::validate, toolset consts, registry helpers all present.
Mode enum {Mcp,Skill,Both,McpLlm,Grammars} matches expected_mcp_args mapping; global --json flag present (main.rs:24) so AC6 dual-format exit code is implementable.
Advisory (non-blocking): harness_serve_surface is Info-only; acceptable since surface is derived and .mcp.json drift is already a hard Fail. No live determine_surface seam — compute inline.
Advisory (non-blocking): ensure explore/standard bool->ModeChoice conversion mirrors serve; decide+test precedence when both flags passed.

implementation.objective: Implement grove doctor with core::harness (sentinel constants), core::doctor (pure diagnose), and Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
key_changes: New core/src/harness.rs: GROVE_START, GROVE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY, expected_mcp_args(Mode), expected_claude_marker(Mode), agents_md_expected(Mode) — single source of truth.
cli/src/init.rs: removed local CLAUDE_START/CLAUDE_END/MCP_SERVER_KEY consts; imported from grove_core::harness — sentinel strings in exactly one place.
New core/src/doctor.rs: Status{Ok,Warn,Fail,Info}, Check, Report, Report::ok(), diagnose(root,force)->Report — pure read-only using core primitives.
core/src/lib.rs: added pub mod harness and pub mod doctor (alphabetically ordered).
Universal checks (11): grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, four harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.
Harness sub-checks compare on-disk state to harness::expected_* functions; Fail on drift with grove init --as <mode> hint.
Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only): explore_config_valid, provider_reachable, model_served, allowed_tools_known, tap_config.
cli/src/main.rs: Cmd::Doctor{path,explore,standard}; grouped human output or --json; exit 1 from Report::ok() under both formats.
Unit tests in harness.rs: 3 coverage tests for all Mode variants across all three functions.
Unit tests in doctor.rs: warn_only exits zero, harness matrix all 5 modes, 4 drift scenarios, lock absent, explore absent, provider unreachable.
Integration tests in cli/tests/cli.rs: doctor --help, clean project exits 0, --json valid, drift exits nonzero (human + json).

code_review.objective: Independently reviewed the grove doctor implementation (core::harness, core::doctor, Cmd::Doctor) against the approved PLAN and task ACs.
key_changes: Isolated true T07 footprint via git diff HEAD: reconcile_harness in the cumulative diff is committed T04 work, not T07.
T07 changes: new harness.rs + doctor.rs, lib.rs module wiring, init.rs constant migration (8 lines, aliased GROVE_START/END), main.rs Cmd::Doctor, cli.rs integration tests.
Verified independently: cargo clippy -D warnings clean; cargo test --release --locked green (177 core + 92 CLI unit + 33 integration + 1 doc, 0 failed).
Read actual harness matrix/drift/explore tests — assertions discriminate real Ok/Warn/Fail, not tautologies.
AC1 shapes exact; Report.mode is core declared Mode not Surface; diagnose pure/read-only.
AC2 mode resolution via shared active_mode(root, force); --explore/--standard precedence correct.
AC3 universal checks present; harness drift -> Fail naming the file with grove init --as <mode> hint.
AC4 lock_integrity delegates to T06 verify_lock: Mismatch->Fail, Missing/absent->Warn (never Fail).
AC5 explore checks gated on McpLlm; HealthError hints surfaced verbatim; allowed_tools warn on unknown.
AC6 Cmd::Doctor + --json dual-format with exit code from Report::ok(); both formats integration-tested.
AC8 build/clippy clean, --help documents doctor, both new files newline-terminated.
Advisory only: Report.mode shows declared (not forced) mode in header; steering legality covered via validate(); cosmetic double blank line in init.rs.

validation.objective: Validate all 10 acceptance criteria for grove doctor: core::harness sentinel constants, core::doctor types/diagnose, universal checks, harness drift detection, lock_integrity, explore-mode checks, Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
key_changes: AC1 PASS: core/src/harness.rs is single source of truth; cli/src/init.rs imports via grove_core::harness alias — no local duplicates.
AC2 PASS: core::doctor exports Status, Check, Report, diagnose — pure read-only verified by source inspection.
AC3 PASS: Doctor dispatch converts --explore/--standard to ModeChoice; diagnose calls active_mode(root,force) internally.
AC4 PASS: All 11 universal checks present: grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, 4 harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.
AC5 PASS: Harness checks Fail on drift with 'grove init --as <mode>' hint; 4 drift unit tests validate specific mismatch scenarios.
AC6 PASS: lock_integrity via verify_lock; absent→Warn (not Fail); Mismatch→Fail; Missing→Warn; unit test confirms boundary.
AC7 PASS: explore_checks gated on Mode::McpLlm; all 5 explore checks present; HealthError hints surfaced verbatim.
AC8 PASS: Cmd::Doctor + global --json + exit 1 from std::process::exit(1); 4 integration tests confirm both output formats.
AC9 PASS: Report::ok() is all(!Fail); warn_only_report_exits_zero unit test directly validates Warn/Info exits 0.
AC10 PASS: clippy clean; 303 tests (177+92+33+1), 0 failed; doctor_help_documents_verb confirms --help coverage.
